name: Via Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas — circular from Villanueva de la Serena
name_original: Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana a Las Villuercas — circular desde Villanueva de la Serena
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Villanueva De La Serena traverse a landscape characterized by expansive plains, pastures dotted with holm oaks, and the fertile areas of the Guadiana River. The region, known as "La Serena," features a mix of granite, quartzite, and slate terrain. Cycling paths often cross small rivers and streams, leading to wetlands and large reservoirs, which are notable for birdwatching. Best touring cycling routes around Villanueva De La Serena The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas — circular from Villanueva de la Serena, a 38.8 miles (62.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route follows a former railway line through pastures, wetlands, and farmland. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Magacela Castle loop from Villanueva de la Serena, a moderate 19.7 miles (31.7 km) path. This route leads to the historic Magacela Castle, offering elevated views. Local touring cyclists also love the Dolmen de Magacela – Villanueva de la Serena loop from Villanueva de la Serena, a 18.4 miles (29.7 km) trail leading through areas with historical sites like the Dolmen de Magacela, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Touring cycling around Villanueva De La Serena is defined by expansive plains, pastures with holm oaks, and routes along the Guadiana River and reservoirs. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Villanueva De La Serena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De La Serena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Villanueva De La Serena?
A: There are 37 touring cycling routes available around Villanueva De La Serena. These routes cater to various preferences and fitness levels, offering a diverse cycling experience.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: The routes around Villanueva De La Serena offer a range of difficulties. You'll find 5 easy routes, 27 moderate routes, and 5 more challenging routes, ensuring options for cyclists of all abilities.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: The terrain is quite varied, characterized by expansive plains and pastures dotted with holm oaks. You'll encounter areas with granite, quartzite, or slate, and routes often cross small rivers and streams leading to wetlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021445416/via-verde-vegas-del-guadiana-to-las-villuercas-circular-from-villanueva-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features a mixed surface of paved and compacted dirt sections.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: Cycling routes often pass through picturesque landscapes featuring the Guadiana River, wetlands, and large reservoirs. The region is known for its holm oak pastures, and during spring and autumn, the scenery is particularly vibrant. You can also explore areas near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5462406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Casas del Montecillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is excellent for birdwatching. The wetlands and large reservoirs, particularly in the eastern part of Extremadura, are a paradise for birds. The area includes a Special Protection Area (ZEPA) in La Serena, where you might spot species like storks, egrets, bee-eaters, plovers, and ibises. There's even a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit on a touring cycling route?
A: Several routes lead to significant historical sites. You can cycle to the well-preserved prehistoric building, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers breathtaking views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129655" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle loop from Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option for this.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: Yes, there are 5 easy routes that are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021445416/via-verde-vegas-del-guadiana-to-las-villuercas-circular-from-villanueva-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted for following a former railway line, offering a relatively flat and scenic ride through pastures and wetlands, making it a great choice for a relaxed family outing.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021445416/via-verde-vegas-del-guadiana-to-las-villuercas-circular-from-villanueva-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas — circular from Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40304711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela – Villanueva de la Serena loop from Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: Spring and autumn are ideal for cycling in Villanueva De La Serena. During these seasons, the landscapes transform with vibrant colors, offering picturesque views, and the weather is generally more pleasant for outdoor activities. Winter also offers unique views of cranes migrating from northern Europe, especially along the Vía Verde.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from expansive plains to holm oak pastures, and the tranquil atmosphere of the region known as 'La Serena'. The variety of routes, including those along the Guadiana River and to historical sites, is also highly appreciated.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by bridges or viewpoints?
A: Yes, you can find routes that feature interesting bridges and viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de la Serena – Ortiga Bridge loop from Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; will take you past the Ortiga Bridge. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic vistas, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Pared&lt;/a&gt; is another notable bridge in the area.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the reservoirs and lakes in the area?
A: Yes, the region is known for its large reservoirs and wetlands. While specific cycling routes may not always directly trace the shorelines, they often pass through areas influenced by these water bodies. You can explore nearby attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Orellana&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana Dam&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the broader lake district around Villanueva de la Serena.
tips: Bridge that allows us to cross to the other side of the Río Guadiana and that is pedestrian. It's worth getting there! It is part of the greenway that goes to Villanueva de la Serena.

An entire stork family has settled here on the old pillars of a ruined building. Maybe you will see a few of them when you cycle past. Attention: Leaning the bike under the nests can result in a new coat of paint.

The landscape through which you cycle on Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana is crossed by several small rivers and streams. The water makes the landscape particularly fertile and forms many small wetlands where you can spot countless rare birds: storks, egrets, bee-eaters, plovers, ibises and much more.

So: don't forget your binoculars and camera!

Villanueva de la Serena (Badajoz, Extremadura) is a dynamic city with much to offer in terms of both history and leisure.
Heritage and Culture

Plaza de España: the city's central square, with historic buildings and a lively atmosphere.
Church of the Assumption: an 18th-century Baroque church, one of the most representative in the area.
Ethnographic Museum: showcases traditional life and local history.
Las Vegas Theatre: a cultural venue with a diverse program.
